context: Tests whether the agent designs the correct four-table cost allocation schema with time-effective cost records, integer-cent monetary storage, returns reserve, variant-level costs, and proper separation of fixed vs variable costs.

checklist.name: Four separate cost tables
checklist.description: Schema includes all four separate tables for the distinct cost categories: one for per-unit product/COGS costs, one for per-order fulfillment costs, one for channel marketing spend, and one for shared overhead allocations

checklist.name: Time-effective cost columns
checklist.description: The product costs table has both an effective_from date column AND an effective_to date column (nullable, indicating currently active when NULL) — not just a single date or timestamp

checklist.name: Integer cents for monetary amounts
checklist.description: Monetary amount columns use INTEGER (or BIGINT) data type with a '_cents' naming convention, NOT DECIMAL, FLOAT, NUMERIC, or MONEY types

checklist.name: Returns reserve column
checklist.description: The per-order fulfillment costs table includes a column for returns reserve (e.g. returns_reserve_cents) as a distinct cost component

checklist.name: Variant-level product costs
checklist.description: The product costs table has a foreign key or reference to product variants (variant_id), not just to products, enabling variant-specific cost records

checklist.name: Overhead allocation_basis column
checklist.description: The overhead allocations table includes an allocation_basis column that records how overhead should be spread (e.g. 'revenue_share', 'order_count', 'unit_count') rather than hard-coding a single allocation method

checklist.name: Per-order fulfillment breakdown
checklist.description: The per-order fulfillment costs table separates at least two variable cost components (e.g. shipping label cost and pick/pack cost as distinct columns) rather than lumping them into one column

checklist.name: Channel marketing attribution columns
checklist.description: The channel marketing costs table includes both a spend amount column AND an orders_attributed (or similar) count column to enable CPA calculation

checklist.name: design_notes mentions cents rationale
checklist.description: design_notes.md explicitly states the reason for using integer cents (avoiding floating-point precision errors in monetary arithmetic)

checklist.name: design_notes mentions historical cost preservation
checklist.description: design_notes.md explicitly explains that cost changes are recorded as new INSERT rows with a new effective_from date rather than updating existing records, to preserve historical margin accuracy
